puffco-new-peak.summary: A countertop electronic dab rig for patients prescribed cannabis concentrates and extracts.
puffco-new-peak.description: The Puffco New Peak is an electronic dab rig that takes the torch and guesswork out of dabbing concentrates. A single button drives four temperature presets, the 3D chamber heats in around 20 seconds, and the glass attachment adds water filtration for a cooler draw. It suits patients who are prescribed extracts rather than dried flower and want consistent, repeatable dosing without a naked flame. It is a sizeable device for home use, not something to carry around — and at this price it only makes sense if concentrates are a regular part of your medicating.

puffco-pivot.summary: A pocket-sized electronic concentrate vaporiser for patients prescribed cannabis extracts on the move.
puffco-pivot.description: The Puffco Pivot brings electronic concentrate vaping into a pen-sized, pocketable form. It uses the same 3D chamber heating as the larger Peak, with four heat settings, an LED bar and haptic feedback so you know where you are without a screen. For patients prescribed extracts who want to medicate discreetly away from home, it is far easier than a torch-and-rig setup. The trade-off is capacity and filtration — it is built for quick, portable sessions rather than the smoother, larger draws of a full desktop rig.

arizer-extreme-q-vaporizer.summary: A versatile desktop dry-herb vaporiser with both balloon-bag and whip delivery, for home use.
arizer-extreme-q-vaporizer.description: The Arizer Extreme Q is a mains-powered desktop vaporiser that gives you two ways to medicate: fill a balloon bag for hands-free dosing, or draw directly through the whip. Convection heating through a glass path keeps flavour clean, and a remote lets you set temperature by the degree from across the room. It suits patients who medicate at home and want flexibility without paying desktop flagship prices. Heat-up takes around two minutes and it is a fixed home device, but for the money the vapour quality is hard to fault.

arizer-v-tower-vaporizer.summary: An affordable whip-style desktop dry-herb vaporiser for patients medicating at home.
arizer-v-tower-vaporizer.description: The Arizer V-Tower is a no-frills mains-powered desktop vaporiser that delivers vapour through a glass whip you draw on directly. Temperature is fully adjustable, the glass vapour path keeps flavour clean, and the heating element carries a lifetime warranty with three years on the electronics. It is one of the most affordable ways into proper convection desktop vaping, which makes it a sensible first home device. There is no balloon-bag option and it stays plugged in on the side, so it is for stationary home use rather than carrying around.

aromed-4-0-vaporizer.summary: A German-built halogen desktop vaporiser with full temperature control and water filtration, for serious home use.
aromed-4-0-vaporizer.description: The AroMed 4.0 is a German-made desktop vaporiser that heats with a halogen bulb and draws through an all-glass vapour path with a built-in water filter for a notably smooth, cool draw. Temperature is fully adjustable across a wide range, and it handles dried herb, oils and extracts. It comes with a five-year warranty and a travel case, and suits committed patients who want a clean glass-and-water vapour path at home. It is a considered purchase at this price, and the glass-heavy design rewards careful handling — but for flavour-focused medicating it is a device people keep for years.

dr-dabber-switch-2.summary: A high-end electronic dab rig using induction heating, for patients prescribed cannabis concentrates.
dr-dabber-switch-2.description: The Dr. Dabber Switch 2 is an electronic dab rig that uses induction heating to bring a quartz insert up to temperature in 5 to 15 seconds, no torch required. Three dynamic heating profiles, app control and precise temperature settings give close control over each draw, and the battery handles up to 50 cycles per charge. It is aimed at patients prescribed concentrates who want flagship dab performance with repeatable dosing. This is a substantial, expensive home device rather than something portable, and it only earns its price if extracts are central to how you medicate.

ispire-the-wand.summary: A torchless induction heater for concentrate bangers and DynaVap caps, for patients who prefer flame-free dabbing.
ispire-the-wand.description: The Ispire Wand is a battery-powered induction heater that brings a borosilicate glass banger up to your chosen temperature without a butane torch. It runs across a wide manual temperature range on two replaceable 18650 batteries, and also heats DynaVap caps if you use one. For patients prescribed concentrates who want consistent, flame-free dabs, it removes the main hazard and guesswork of torch dabbing. The body is plastic and it pairs with a separate rig or DynaVap rather than working alone, so think of it as the heat source in a wider setup.

plenty-vaporizer.summary: A handheld mains-powered hybrid vaporiser from Storz & Bickel, for powerful home sessions.
plenty-vaporizer.description: The Plenty is Storz & Bickel's corded handheld vaporiser, sitting between the portable Mighty and the desktop Volcano. Hybrid convection-and-conduction heating through a large filling chamber and a stainless-steel cooling coil produces dense, cool vapour, with temperature set on an analogue dial. It suits patients who medicate at home and want serious vapour output without managing a battery. It is tethered to the mains and on the bulky side, so it is a home device — but the cooling coil makes for some of the smoothest draws in the range.

stundenglass-modul-dry-material-tank.summary: A replacement quartz heating tank for the Stündenglass Modül vaporiser.
stundenglass-modul-dry-material-tank.description: This is a replacement tank for the Stündenglass Modül, with a full quartz chamber and a stainless-steel heating element for even heating of dried material or concentrate. The magnetic connection ring clicks securely onto the Modül for quick swaps. It is a spare or replacement part rather than a standalone device, useful for keeping a Modül running or switching cleanly between materials. You will need the Modül vaporiser itself for this to do anything.

stundenglass-modul-vaporizer-6000mah.summary: A 6000mAh vaporiser attachment that turns a Stündenglass water pipe into a temperature-controlled vaporiser.
stundenglass-modul-vaporizer-6000mah.description: The Stündenglass Modül is a battery-powered vaporiser attachment that pairs with the Stündenglass or Kompact water pipe to vaporise dried herb or concentrate through water. A quartz heating chamber, dial control and full-colour screen give precise temperature and session control, and the 6000mAh battery manages up to 50 sessions per charge with pass-through use. It suits patients who already own a Stündenglass pipe and want to add proper temperature-controlled vapour. It needs that compatible water pipe to work, so it is an upgrade to an existing setup rather than a complete device on its own.

vapman-heating-station.summary: A mains-powered heating dock for the Vapman vaporiser, replacing the butane torch with controlled electric heat.
vapman-heating-station.description: The Vapman Heating Station is a desktop dock built for the Vapman vaporiser, swapping the usual butane flame for controlled electric heat. It reaches vaporising temperature in 3 to 5 seconds across an adjustable 160 to 230°C range, with automatic shut-off, and is hand-assembled from olive wood, stainless steel, aluminium and glass. It suits Vapman owners who want repeatable temperatures and no torch on the side. It only heats the Vapman, so it is an accessory for existing owners rather than a vaporiser in itself, and the materials make it a considered purchase.

volcano-vaporizer-classic.summary: The original German-built balloon-bag desktop vaporiser from Storz & Bickel, for hands-free home dosing.
volcano-vaporizer-classic.description: The Volcano Classic is Storz & Bickel's long-running desktop vaporiser, filling a detachable balloon bag with vapour you then medicate from hands-free. Convection heating through a food-grade aluminium block and an all-metal body give it a reputation for reliability that runs into years, with temperature set on a simple dial. It suits patients who want consistent, hands-free dosing at home and value a device that lasts. Heat-up is slower at around three minutes and it is a heavy mains-only unit, but it is one of the most durable home vaporisers made and carries a three-year warranty.

dynavap-m7.summary: A butane-heated, battery-free dry-herb vaporiser with a stainless-steel build, for patients who want flavour and simplicity.
dynavap-m7.description: The DynaVap M7 is a torch-heated dry-herb vaporiser with no electronics and no battery to worry about. The redesigned stainless-steel tip holds more heat for greater control and bigger draws, while the finless design reduces scorching and hot spots. It is forgiving enough for newcomers yet rewards practice, making it a sensible first step into the DynaVap world. You will need a butane torch (sold separately) and a moment to learn the click-to-temperature rhythm — but in return you get clean flavour, no charging, and a device that keeps working for years.

dynavap-the-b.summary: An affordable butane-heated dry-herb vaporiser with a silicone stem, for patients new to the DynaVap method.
dynavap-the-b.description: The DynaVap B is the most affordable way into the DynaVap range, pairing a single-finned stainless-steel tip with a food-grade silicone stem. It heats with a butane torch, needs no battery, and the signature cap clicks to tell you when it is at temperature. The small 0.1g chamber suits microdosing and conserves material, which makes it a low-cost, low-commitment first vaporiser. You will need a torch (sold separately) and a little practice with the technique, but the flavour and simplicity are the same DynaVap appeal at a lower price.

dynavap-the-b2.summary: A durable butane-heated dry-herb vaporiser in medical-grade stainless steel and silicone, for everyday use.
dynavap-the-b2.description: The DynaVap B2 is a torch-heated dry-herb vaporiser built from medical-grade stainless steel and food-safe silicone for daily, knock-about use. It needs no battery and produces clean, combustion-free vapour that brings out the flavour of dried flower. The compact build is pocketable and discreet, and it conserves material well, making it efficient for regular medicating. As with all DynaVaps you will need a butane torch (sold separately) and a short learning curve to find your rhythm — the reward is a robust device with nothing to charge.

dynavap-the-g3.summary: A low-cost glass-bodied butane-heated vaporiser for microdosing, ideal as a first DynaVap.
dynavap-the-g3.description: The DynaVap G3 is an affordable, torch-powered vaporiser with a one-piece borosilicate glass body joining the tip and midsection. The small 0.05g chamber is built for microdosing — efficient and discreet — and the kit arrives with a metal case and a jet lighter, so you can start straight away. It needs no battery and delivers the clean, flavour-led vapour DynaVap is known for. Note it is not compatible with induction heaters, so it is for patients happy with manual torch heating and a little practice on the technique.

dynavap-the-hyperdyn.summary: A large-chamber titanium butane-heated vaporiser for patients who want fuller DynaVap sessions.
dynavap-the-hyperdyn.description: The DynaVap HyperDyn is the big-chamber model in the range, with a roughly 0.25g titanium bowl for fuller sessions than the standard tips allow. It is built from medical-grade titanium with DuraDyn protection, and its 10mm and 14mm tapers let you draw through water pieces for a cooler hit. It stays compatible with existing DynaVap midsections, tips and mouthpieces, and includes a 0.125g dosing capsule for smaller doses. It still needs a butane torch (sold separately) and the same click-to-temperature technique — this is the choice for patients who already know DynaVap and want a larger capacity.

dynavap-the-unidyn-ball-vape.summary: A titanium one-piece ball-vape from DynaVap with adjustable airflow, for flavour-focused patients.
dynavap-the-unidyn-ball-vape.description: The DynaVap UniDyn is a torch-heated ball vaporiser built as a single titanium piece, joining tip and stem to cut down on parts and add durability. Its BallR cap and convection heating focus on clean flavour extraction, while seven airflow settings and an adjustable chamber let you switch between microdosing and a full session. The extended length cools the vapour for a smoother draw. As with every DynaVap it needs a butane torch (sold separately) and a little practice, but it suits flavour-led patients who want fine control over each session with minimal maintenance.
